text:
There was a curious little fox who lived in a quiet forest.

One morning he saw a beautiful golden feather floating down into a babbling brook.

Wanting it so much, he dove after it.

But then the feather twinged.

His head spun.

He swooped down again.

But that night a hawk took the feather.

The little fox dreamed he flew up and took the hawk's eye.

Now he could see!

He swooped down.

Swooped right into the hawk's talons.

That night an owl stole them both.

But in his dreams, the fox shook loose another feather.

This time an owl loved birdsilk.

That autumn an owl took him flying.

They sat together on a branch.

Just little Owls.

Just little friends.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
